channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: As ceasefire deadline approaches, tensions between the U.S. and Iran continue to rise
item.description: What are the political stakes for President Trump ahead of a second round of planned negotiations to end the war with Iran?
item.content:encoded: What are the political stakes for President Trump ahead of a second round of planned negotiations to end the war with Iran?
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: What's next for the U.S. and Iran as each accuses the other of violating ceasefire?
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin talks to Richard Nephew, former deputy special envoy for Iran in the Biden administration, about paths for diplomacy as the U.S. and Iran accuse each other of ceasefire violations.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin talks to Richard Nephew, former deputy special envoy for Iran in the Biden administration, about paths for diplomacy as the U.S. and Iran accuse each other of ceasefire violations.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Tensions with Trump continue to follow the pope as he continues his Africa tour
item.description: As Pope Leo continues his 11-day tour of Africa, the visit is being overshadowed by tensions with Washington.
item.content:encoded: As Pope Leo continues his 11-day tour of Africa, the visit is being overshadowed by tensions with Washington.
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Akinwotu

item.title: Got wearable data? Your doctor can help you connect the dots
item.description: The American Academy of Neurology issued guidance on using wearable data devices, like smartwatches or an Oura Ring, to track key health metrics that can help flag serious conditions.
item.content:encoded:
The American Academy of Neurology issued guidance on using wearable data devices, like smartwatches or an Oura Ring, to track key health metrics that can help flag serious conditions.

(Image credit: Natalia Lebedinskaia)
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump announces planned Iran war peace talks, Tehran signals it may boycott negotiations amid ongoing U.S. naval pressure, businesses can now apply for Trump tariff refunds.
item.content:encoded: Trump announces planned Iran war peace talks, Tehran signals it may boycott negotiations amid ongoing U.S. naval pressure, businesses can now apply for Trump tariff refunds.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Journalist discusses the history behind the conflict between Israel and Lebanon
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with journalist Kim Ghattas about the long history of conflict between Israel and Lebanon.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with journalist Kim Ghattas about the long history of conflict between Israel and Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: 8 children killed in mass shooting in Louisiana, police say
item.description: Eight children between the ages of 1 and 14 are dead after a mass shooting in Louisiana, police say.
item.content:encoded: Eight children between the ages of 1 and 14 are dead after a mass shooting in Louisiana, police say.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: FBI staffers raise concerns about Director Kash Patel's behavior, report says
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks to Sarah Fitzpatrick, staff writer at The Atlantic, about concerns raised by FBI staff members about the leadership of Director Kash Patel.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks to Sarah Fitzpatrick, staff writer at The Atlantic, about concerns raised by FBI staff members about the leadership of Director Kash Patel.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: After massive cuts, U.S. says HIV work abroad is going well, but experts disagree
item.description: The Trump administration has released new data on the country's HIV/AIDS work abroad. It is claiming the numbers are good news, but many HIV experts say they paint a worrisome picture.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration has released new data on the country's HIV/AIDS work abroad. It is claiming the numbers are good news, but many HIV experts say they paint a worrisome picture.
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Ukraine's fast-growing defense tech sector takes center stage in Iran war
item.description: While Ukraine seeks to shift its image from not only a recipient of military aid but also a provider, its battle-tested, anti-drone tech is taking center stage in the Iran war.
item.content:encoded: While Ukraine seeks to shift its image from not only a recipient of military aid but also a provider, its battle-tested, anti-drone tech is taking center stage in the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis
